auth_type: Varies (Basic Auth, Static API Keys, or Session Cookies)
auth_notes: Legacy systems often lack OAuth2. They typically rely on Basic Auth, IP whitelisting, or custom header-based tokens. Some may require a multi-step login to obtain a session cookie before making functional calls.
rate_limits: Usually undefined or very low; legacy hardware may fail under concurrent requests.
gotchas: Payloads are often in XML/SOAP format rather than JSON, requiring heavy use of the XML node or Code node.
Lack of standard error codes; systems may return 200 OK even when a functional error occurs.
Inconsistent data types and lack of pagination support for large datasets.
client_must_provide: Technical documentation (often in PDF or Word format)
VPN access or firewall whitelisting for n8n instance IP
Test environment credentials and sample request/response payloads
operations_available: Direct Database Querying (if API is unavailable)
research_notes: Integration will primarily use the HTTP Request node. Expect to use the 'Code' node to transform legacy data structures (like flat files or nested XML) into clean JSON for n8n. If the system is behind a corporate firewall, an n8n binary install or a tunnel/VPN will be mandatory.

effort_recommendation.rationale: Integration will primarily use the HTTP Request node. Expect to use the 'Code' node to transform legacy data structures (like flat files or nested XML) into clean JSON for n8n. If the system is behind a corporate firewall, an n8n binary install or a tunnel/VPN will be mandatory.
